Ntc Of Petition And Petition For Order To Arbitrate And For Dismissal Or Stay Of Action Pending Arbitration
Off Calendar Sep-16-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
CROSS-DEFENDANT WAUKEEN Q. MCCOY'S PETITION FOR ORDER TO ARBITRATE AND FOR DISMISSAL OR STAY OF ACTION PENDING ARBITRATION.

RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - denied. This action involves a third party and claims not subject to arbitration and there is a possibility of conflicting rulings on common issues of law or fact. C.C.P. section 1281.2(c). (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-07-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
CROSS-DEFENDANT WAUKEEN Q. MCCOY'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF AND FOR DAMAGES BY CROSS-COMPLAINANT ANDREW EHRENFELD.

APPEARANCES: Spencer Smith, Esq. of Law Offices of Waukeen Q. McCoy 675-7705 for deft/x-deft & moving party; Bruce M. Glassner, Esq. of Gwire Law Offices 296-8880 for x-complainant & opposing party.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - sustained without leave to amend. No reasonable probability that defect can cured by amendment. The court takes judicial notice of the agreement between cross-complainant and McCoy pursuant to E.C. section 452(h). Whether McCoy owed a duty to advise cross-complainant of and pursue a malpractice claim against Goldstein is a question of law, not fact. Nichols v. Keller (1993) 15 Cal. App. 4th 1672, 1682. The court finds that under the retainer agreement, McCoy did not owe a duty to file a malpractice claim or advise of the statute of limitations on the claim. McCoy was hired to help cross-complanaint successfully resolve the underlying suit. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-23-2010 Continued To Oct-22-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-26-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: CROSS DEFENDANT GOLDSTEIN, GELLMAN, MELBOSTAD, GIBSON & HARRIS'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT OF ANDREW EHRENFELD is sustained without leave to amend, no reasonable probability that defect can be cured by amendment. Declaratory relief cause of action appears to allege malpractice against Goldstein which is time barred. Cause of action addresses 2 separate agreements. Cross-complainant's other cause of action adequately addresses claim for indemnity. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
